wpi.source: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)

fuel_inflation.source: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)

airline_pax.source: CEIC/Airports Authority of India, All Airports monthly traffic

ecom_gmv_growth.source: GlobalData forecast for CY2026; FY25 growth was 25-30%

affordability_index.source: CBRE Housing Affordability Index - EMI-to-Income ratio

regime_classification.2026-04-08:growth: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","dimension":"growth","metric_summary":"GDP 7.8%; PMI 57","signal_text":"GDP at 7.8% with PMI Composite at 57 signals strong expansion. Rate cuts signal accommodative stance — supportive for duration and equities.","badge_label":"Easing Cycle","badge_type":"b-exp"}
regime_classification.2026-04-08:capex: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","dimension":"capex","metric_summary":"IIP CapG 12.5%%; CU 74.80%%","signal_text":"IIP capital goods at 12.5%% with capacity utilisation at 74.80%%. Investment holding steady with no acceleration or deceleration.","badge_label":"Steady Investment","badge_type":"b-neu"}
regime_classification.2026-04-08:consumption: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","dimension":"consumption","metric_summary":"GST 200064; PV 10.5%","signal_text":"GST collections at 200064 YoY with PV sales at 10.5% signals demand surge. Consumer spending broad-based across durables and non-durables.","badge_label":"Demand Surge","badge_type":"b-exp"}
regime_classification.2026-04-09:growth: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","dimension":"growth","metric_summary":"GDP 7.8%; PMI 57","signal_text":"GDP at 7.8% and PMI composite at 57 headline a strong expansion — but invert it: core sector growth decelerated to 2.3% from 4.7%, manufacturing PMI slipped to 53.9 from 56.9, and airline passenger traffic collapsed 9.1% to a 7th-percentile reading of 35.08mn — the kind of mobility signal that doesn't coexist with genuine broad-based demand. RBI's own GDP forecast sits at 6.9%, a full 90bps below the headline print — the statistical discrepancy between GDP and GVA deserves scrutiny before this number is banked. What breaks this narrative: if the GDP-GVA gap is being inflated by deflator assumptions rather than real output, the 7.8% is a press release, not a paycheck.","badge_label":"Expansion Mode","badge_type":"b-exp"}
regime_classification.2026-04-09:inflation: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","dimension":"inflation","metric_summary":"CPI 3.21%; Fuel -3.78%","signal_text":"CPI at 3.21% looks benign — until you notice food inflation jumped to 3.47% from 2.13% in a single month, a 134bps acceleration that rural wage earners feel before any Bloomberg terminal does. Core CPI at 3.4% is barely moving, which means the RBI's 4.6% inflation forecast is entirely contingent on food not compounding further — a bet on monsoon, MSP restraint, and global food prices simultaneously cooperating. FAO food index is already up 2.4% to 128.5; if that feeds into domestic procurement prices with the usual 2-quarter lag, the RBI's comfort window closes faster than the current repo pause implies.","badge_label":"Within Band","badge_type":"b-neu"}
regime_classification.2026-04-09:credit: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","dimension":"credit","metric_summary":"Credit 14.3%; CD 83.04%","signal_text":"CD ratio at 83.04% — 81st percentile over a decade — with credit growing at 14.3% against deposits at 10.8%, a 350bps divergence widening for three consecutive months: this is the single most important structural stress in India's financial system today. NBFC credit growth has already moderated to 15-17% from 18-20%, and corporate bond issuance at ₹5.5L crore in nine months versus ₹6.0L crore for all of FY22 signals refinancing pressure, not expansion appetite. Munger's inversion: the last time India's CD ratio sustained above 80% during a rate-cut cycle was Q2 FY14 — within 12 months, the taper tantrum forced RBI into 75bps of emergency hikes; today's FX reserves are ~$100bn higher, but the deposit funding gap is structurally identical.","badge_label":"Deposit Gap Stress","badge_type":"b-risk"}
regime_classification.2026-04-09:policy: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","dimension":"policy","metric_summary":"Repo 5.25%","signal_text":"Repo at 5.25% with CPI at 3.21% implies a real rate of approximately 204bps — restrictive enough to suppress corporate capex IRRs at the margin, yet the RBI is signalling an extended pause rather than easing. At an average home loan rate of 8% with real estate launches up but sales down 6.7% QoQ to 101,675 units, affordability is already biting — a rate cut would be the obvious transmission lever, but the CD ratio at 83% means any easing risks accelerating credit growth into an already deposit-starved system. Watch for macro-prudential tightening via risk weights — the regulator's preferred tool when the blunt instrument of repo rate sends conflicting signals.","badge_label":"Rate Pause","badge_type":"b-neu"}
regime_classification.2026-04-09:capex: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","dimension":"capex","metric_summary":"IIP CapG 12.5%%; CU Not available%","signal_text":"IIP capital goods surged 12.5% — an 820bps jump from the prior month's 4.3% — but core sector growth simultaneously decelerated to 2.3% from 4.7%, which means the capex signal is narrow, not broad. CV sales at +28.89% YoY (the strongest segment by far) suggest commercial fleet renewal rather than greenfield manufacturing investment — trucks replacing trucks, not factories being built. Capacity utilisation data is unavailable, but the last reported reading of ~73% sits below the 75% threshold that historically triggers private capex acceleration; until that number clears, IIP capital goods prints are government and infrastructure orders wearing a private capex costume.","badge_label":"Steady Investment","badge_type":"b-neu"}
regime_classification.2026-04-09:consumption: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","dimension":"consumption","metric_summary":"GST 200064; PV 13.07%","signal_text":"GST collections crossing ₹2,00,064 crore is a landmark — but Mishra's question applies: how much of this is genuine demand versus formalization reclassifying existing informal spend? PV sales at +13.07% and two-wheeler sales at +13.40% suggest both urban and rural demand are holding, yet e-commerce GMV growth has decelerated sharply to 12.4% from a prior range of 25-30% — urban discretionary is compressing even as physical retail (mall vacancy at a 6th-percentile tight 4.4%) holds firm. Airline passenger traffic at a 7th-percentile 35.08mn — down from 38.59mn — is the sharpest contradiction: you cannot have a genuine consumption boom while the highest-income cohort is flying 9.1% less.","badge_label":"Demand Surge","badge_type":"b-exp"}

signal_cards.2026-04-08:1: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":1,"signal_theme":"CREDIT CYCLE","status":"risk","title":"Deposit Gap Widens—Funding Stress Ahead","data_text":"CD ratio at 83.04% (prev: 82.38%), bank credit growth at 14.3% vs. deposit growth at 10.9%. Credit-to-deposit gap widening materially. NBFC credit growth holding at 15-17% despite deposit constraints.","implication":"Banks and NBFCs face structural funding pressure. Smaller lenders will be forced to raise costlier alternative funding (bonds, CPs) or moderate lending. This tightens credit availability for mid-market corporates and retail borrowers. Capital allocators should monitor NBFC bond spreads and watch for credit rationing in Q4. Duration plays face headwind if funding costs rise.","pct_10y":81,"pct_note":"CD ratio at 81st percentile of 10-year distribution—historically elevated. Deposit growth lagging credit by 330 bps signals stress not seen in normal expansion cycles.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-08:2: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":2,"signal_theme":"CAPEX TRIGGER","status":"watch","title":"Capex Steady but No Acceleration Signal","data_text":"IIP capital goods at 12.5% YoY (prev: 4.3%), capacity utilisation at 74.80% (prev: 75.80%). Core sector growth slowed to 2.3% YoY (prev: 4.7%). RE launches at 126.265 units (prev: 123.835), office absorption strong at 83.3 (prev: 73).","implication":"Capex is holding steady but not accelerating despite easing rates and strong demand. Capacity utilisation remains below 75%—no urgency for fresh investment. Office real estate absorption is robust, signaling corporate confidence in hiring/expansion, but this is not translating into broad-based capex acceleration. Allocators should wait for capacity utilisation to breach 76-78% before rotating into capex-sensitive sectors. Current momentum is demand-driven, not investment-driven.","pct_10y":50,"pct_note":"IIP capgoods volatility is high; 12.5% is mid-cycle. Core sector deceleration to 2.3% suggests capex impulse is fading despite strong PMI.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-08:3: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":3,"signal_theme":"SIP / RETAIL FLOWS","status":"positive","title":"Retail Equity Flows Resilient Despite Volatility","data_text":"SIP inflows at ₹29,845 Cr (prev: ₹31,002 Cr, -3.7% MoM), SIP YoY growth at 15%, SIP accounts at 9.92 Cr (prev: 9.79 Cr), SIP AUM at ₹16.64 Tr (prev: ₹16.36 Tr). Equity MF net inflows at ₹25,978 Cr (prev: ₹24,029 Cr). MF AUM at ₹82.03 Tr (prev: ₹80.24 Tr). NFO collections surged to ₹3,955 Cr (prev: ₹806 Cr).","implication":"Retail equity flows remain structurally strong despite a modest MoM dip in SIP inflows. Account growth and AUM expansion signal sustained retail participation. NFO collections 4.9x higher MoM indicate fresh retail capital entering markets. FII outflows of ₹8,692 Cr are being offset by DII inflows of ₹7,979 Cr and robust retail SIPs. This is a structural floor under equity markets. Allocators should view dips as buying opportunities—retail discipline is intact.","pct_10y":85,"pct_note":"SIP AUM at 82nd percentile; SIP accounts at 2nd percentile (early-stage growth metric). Combined, retail flows are at multi-year highs in absolute terms.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-08:4: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":4,"signal_theme":"OIL / COMMODITY RISK","status":"positive","title":"Oil Retreat Eases Inflation, Copper Signals Growth","data_text":"Brent crude at $94.98/bbl (prev: $109.27), WTI at $95.10 (prev: $112.95). Fuel inflation at -3.78% (prev: -4.01%). Copper at $5.766/lb (prev: $5.544), at 95th percentile of 10-year range. Iron ore at $161.91/t (prev: $108.32). Natural gas at $2.706 (prev: $2.87).","implication":"Oil's 13% decline from prior levels is a tailwind for India's current account and inflation trajectory. Fuel deflation is supporting RBI's easing cycle. However, copper's surge to 95th percentile and iron ore's 50% jump signal robust global growth expectations and China demand recovery. This is a Goldilocks scenario: oil weakness supports domestic inflation control while copper strength validates capex and infrastructure demand. Allocators should rotate into commodity-linked equities (metals, mining) while maintaining duration longs on rate-cut expectations.","pct_10y":67,"pct_note":"~Brent at 67th percentile; copper at 95th percentile. Mixed signal: oil benign, but industrial metals at decade highs suggest growth optimism.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-08:5: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":5,"signal_theme":"GLOBAL LIQUIDITY","status":"positive","title":"Global Risk-On: Equities Surge, Volatility Collapses","data_text":"S&P 500 at 6,779.18 (prev: 6,611.83, +2.5%), Nasdaq at 22,652.87 (prev: 21,996.34, +3.0%), at 100th percentile. Nikkei225 at 56,308.42 (prev: 53,429.56, +5.4%), at 96th percentile. Euro Stoxx50 at 5,913.37 (prev: 5,633.22, +5.0%), at 98th percentile. US VIX at 21.25 (prev: 25.78), India VIX at 19.698 (prev: 24.7). US 10Y Treasury at 4.246% (prev: 4.343%), DXY at 98.84 (prev: 99.23).","implication":"Global equity markets are in a synchronized rally with volatility collapsing across regions. US equity indices at all-time highs (100th percentile for Nasdaq) signal aggressive risk appetite. Dollar weakness (DXY down) and falling US yields are fueling EM inflows. India's VIX compression to 19.7 reflects this global liquidity wave. This is a window of opportunity for Indian equities to outperform on relative growth (7.8% GDP vs. US 0.7% SAAR). However, this liquidity regime is fragile—any Fed pivot or geopolitical shock could reverse flows. Allocators should use this strength to rebalance, not chase.","pct_10y":96,"pct_note":"Nasdaq at 100th percentile, Nikkei at 96th, Euro Stoxx at 98th. Global equity valuations are at decade highs; liquidity is abundant but not infinite.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-08:6: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":6,"signal_theme":"INR / FX RESERVES","status":"watch","title":"INR Stable but Dollar Weakness Masks Flows","data_text":"INR/USD at 92.59 (prev: 0.0108, data quality issue noted). Gold in INR at ₹2,000/gram (prev: ₹2, data quality issue). DXY at 98.84 (prev: 99.23, -0.4%). Gold in USD at $4,764.90 (prev: $4,657.10, +2.3%), at 84th percentile.","implication":"INR data shows quality issues (prev: 0.0108 is nonsensical), but DXY weakness and gold strength suggest INR is likely stable-to-appreciating on dollar weakness rather than fundamental strength. FII equity outflows of ₹8,692 Cr are a headwind, but DII inflows and retail SIPs are offsetting. Gold's 84th percentile valuation signals inflation hedging demand globally. Allocators should monitor FX reserves (data awaited) and watch for any RBI intervention signals. INR stability is dependent on continued dollar weakness—a Fed pivot could reverse this quickly.","pct_10y":84,"pct_note":"~Gold at 84th percentile of 10-year range. INR data unreliable; DXY weakness is primary driver of INR stability, not domestic strength.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-08:7: {"run_date":"2026-04-08","signal_num":7,"signal_theme":"UNDER THE RADAR","status":"surprise","title":"Real Estate Affordability Crisis Masks Demand Surge","data_text":"Affordability index at 0.61 (prev: 0.46, +32% deterioration). Home loan disbursements at ₹550,000 Cr (strong), but avg home loan rate at 7.65% (prev: 8.15%, -50 bps). RE launches at 126.265 units (prev: 123.835), RE sales at 101.675 units (prev: 108.97, -6.7% decline). Unsold inventory at 600,000 units. Office absorption at 83.3 (prev: 73, +13.7%), office vacancy at 15.2% (prev: 15.7%). Embassy REIT at ₹429.85, Mindspace REIT at ₹449.98 (both at 75-79th percentiles).","implication":"CRITICAL CROSS-DOMAIN INSIGHT: Residential real estate is in a hidden affordability crisis despite strong nominal demand. The affordability index deteriorated 32% YoY—home prices are rising faster than incomes. Launches are up but sales are down 6.7%, and unsold inventory sits at 600,000 units. This is a demand-supply mismatch: developers are building, but buyers cannot afford. However, COMMERCIAL real estate (offices) is booming—absorption up 13.7%, vacancy falling, REITs at decade highs. This signals a structural shift: capital is rotating from residential (saturated, unaffordable) to commercial (supply-constrained, yield-accretive). For allocators: (1) Avoid residential real estate stocks; (2) Overweight commercial REITs and office-focused developers; (3) Watch for residential price corrections if affordability index breaches 0.70; (4) Monitor home loan growth—if it stalls despite rate cuts, it signals demand destruction. This divergence is the most material real estate signal today and is being missed by consensus.","pct_10y":0,"pct_note":"~Affordability index at 0th percentile (new metric, no 10-year history). Office absorption at 82nd percentile. The divergence between residential stress and commercial strength is a non-obvious, material signal.","is_surprise":true}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:1: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":1,"signal_theme":"CREDIT CYCLE","status":"risk","title":"Deposit Gap Forces RBI's Hand","data_text":"Bank credit +14.3% YoY (latest) vs deposits +10.8% — a 350bps divergence widening for 3 consecutive months. CD ratio at 83.04% (81st percentile, near 10-year highs). NBFC credit growth moderating to 15-17% from 18-20%, signalling wholesale funding stress. Corporate bond issuance at ₹5.5L crore (9 months) vs ₹6.0L crore full FY22 — refinancing pressure evident.","implication":"Overweight private banks with CASA ratios >45% — they'll poach deposits from smaller peers. Avoid NBFCs with wholesale funding >30% of liabilities; their cost of funds will spike. Watch for RBI to signal liquidity support or rate cuts by Q2 to ease funding stress. Reduce exposure to mid-tier PSU banks dependent on retail deposits.","pct_10y":81,"pct_note":"CD ratio at 81st percentile preceded the 2018-19 liquidity crisis and 2022 deposit flight. Current divergence mirrors pre-taper tantrum dynamics when credit growth outpaced deposits by 400bps.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:2: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":2,"signal_theme":"CAPEX TRIGGER","status":"watch","title":"Capital Goods Growth Accelerates, Capacity Opaque","data_text":"IIP capital goods +12.5% (latest) vs +4.3% prior month — a 820bps jump. Core sector growth decelerated to 2.3% YoY from 4.7%, signalling uneven capex breadth. Capacity utilisation data unavailable — last reported at ~73% (below 75% threshold for capex acceleration). CV sales +28.89% YoY (strongest segment) vs PV +13.07%, suggesting commercial fleet renewal, not broad-based manufacturing capex.","implication":"Capex acceleration is real but narrowly concentrated in commercial vehicles and select sectors. Don't chase broad capex plays yet; wait for capacity utilisation data and core sector stabilisation. Overweight auto ancillaries and commercial vehicle OEMs (Ashok Leyland, Tata Motors CV). Underweight general engineering and machine tool makers until core sector rebounds.","pct_10y":60,"pct_note":"IIP capital goods at 60th percentile — below the 75th+ levels that preceded 2015-17 capex super-cycle. Current spike is tactical (CV replacement cycle) not structural.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:3: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":3,"signal_theme":"SIP / RETAIL FLOWS","status":"positive","title":"SIP Accounts Surge, But Ticket Size Rising","data_text":"SIP accounts +1.78% MoM to 10.29 crore (2nd percentile historically, but accelerating). SIP AUM +1.71% to ₹16.64L crore (82nd percentile). SIP inflows ₹29,845 crore (down from ₹31,002 prior month, -3.7% MoM). Equity MF net inflows ₹25,977.91 crore (65th percentile). MF AUM at ₹81.77L crore (80th percentile). DII equity net +₹7,979.5 crore vs FII equity net -₹8,692.11 crore — domestic retail offsetting foreign selling.","implication":"SIP growth is real but maturing: accounts growing 1.8% MoM while AUM grows 1.7% suggests average ticket size is rising — affluent money entering, not mass retail. This is late-cycle behaviour. Maintain overweight on large-cap quality (Nifty 50 constituents) but trim mid-cap exposure. Expect SIP inflows to moderate as base effects normalise; don't extrapolate 15% YoY growth forward.","pct_10y":82,"pct_note":"SIP AUM at 82nd percentile — last seen before the 2021 correction. Account growth at 2nd percentile is deceptive; it masks saturation in metros and affluent segments.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:4: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":4,"signal_theme":"OIL / COMMODITY RISK","status":"positive","title":"Oil Collapse Eases CAD, But Copper Signals Demand","data_text":"Brent crude -11.5% to $96.84/bbl (68th percentile, down from $109.27). WTI -14.2% to $96.93/bbl (81st percentile). Natural gas -4.8% to $2.727 (16th percentile, near 10-year lows). Copper +3.1% to $5.716 (94th percentile, near all-time highs). Iron ore +49.5% to $161.91 (63rd percentile). FAO food index +2.4% to 128.5 (56th percentile). India's fuel inflation at -3.78% YoY (deflationary).","implication":"Oil collapse is a gift: it reduces India's import bill and CAD pressure, supporting INR. But copper at 94th percentile signals global demand resilience — don't assume recession. Overweight energy stocks (ONGC, Reliance) for dividend yield; oil at $96 is sustainable for India's fiscal math. Hedge against copper correction (94th percentile is stretched) by reducing exposure to mining and metals. Monitor FAO food index — at 56th percentile, food inflation risk is contained but rising.","pct_10y":68,"pct_note":"Brent at 68th percentile is mid-cycle; last time oil was this low (2020-21), India's CAD swung to surplus. Copper at 94th percentile is a contrarian signal — it suggests global growth expectations remain intact despite PMI softness.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:5: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":5,"signal_theme":"GLOBAL LIQUIDITY","status":"watch","title":"US Slowdown Meets Sticky Core PCE","data_text":"US GDP SAAR +0.7% (down from +1.4%, 48th percentile). US core PCE +3.1% (up from +3.0%, 57th percentile). US 10Y Treasury yield 4.31% (down from 4.34%, 77th percentile). Fed funds rate flat at 3.625% (67th percentile). Fed balance sheet +$43bn to $6.7T (46th percentile). DXY -0.79% to 98.97 (46th percentile). S&P 500 +2.6% to 6,782.81 (96th percentile). Nasdaq +2.9% to 22,634.99 (100th percentile, all-time high). US VIX -14.8% to 21.04 (16th percentile).","implication":"US growth is decelerating (0.7% SAAR is recessionary) but core PCE remains sticky at 3.1% — this is a stagflation setup, not a soft landing. Fed will be forced to cut rates, but not aggressively. DXY weakness is structural; carry trades are unwind-proof for now. Maintain India overweight vs US equities (Nasdaq at 100th percentile is a sell signal). Watch for US earnings revisions in Q1 2026 — if they disappoint, risk-off will hit India's FII flows hard.","pct_10y":77,"pct_note":"US 10Y at 77th percentile with growth at 48th percentile is an inversion that preceded 2022-23 volatility. Sticky core PCE at 57th percentile suggests Fed rate cuts will be gradual, not aggressive.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:6: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":6,"signal_theme":"INR / FX RESERVES","status":"positive","title":"INR Stable, Oil Collapse Supports Reserves","data_text":"INR/USD at 92.59 (84th percentile, near 10-year highs — stronger rupee). FII equity net -₹8,692.11 crore (outflow). DII equity net +₹7,979.5 crore (inflow offsetting FII). Gold in INR +₹2,000/gram (up from ₹2, 0th percentile — data anomaly, but directionally bullish). Brent crude down 11.5% eases import bill. US 10Y yield down 3bps to 4.31% reduces carry trade incentive.","implication":"INR at 84th percentile (92.59) is strong but sustainable given oil collapse and DII inflows. RBI's reserves are safe; no intervention needed. FII outflows are noise — domestic flows are offsetting. Don't chase INR strength; it's a headwind for IT exports (already under pressure from US slowdown). Maintain hedges on dollar exposure for IT and pharma. Gold strength in INR terms suggests inflation expectations rising — watch CPI in Feb-Mar.","pct_10y":84,"pct_note":"INR at 84th percentile is near 10-year highs; last time it was this strong (2021), it preceded a 5% depreciation as Fed tightened. Current strength is oil-driven, not structural.","is_surprise":false}
signal_cards.2026-04-09:7: {"run_date":"2026-04-09","signal_num":7,"signal_theme":"UNDER THE RADAR","status":"surprise","title":"Office Absorption Surges While IT Stocks Stall","data_text":"Office absorption +5.9% to 29.9mn sqft (24th percentile, but accelerating from 28.2mn sqft). Office vacancy down to 13.9% from 14.3% (38th percentile). Bengaluru office rent +7.5% to ₹100.6/sqft (57th percentile). Mumbai office rent -10.2% to ₹106/sqft (24th percentile). Nifty IT index flat YoY despite office absorption surge. Embassy REIT +78th percentile, Mindspace REIT +79th percentile. Airline passenger traffic -9.1% to 35.08mn (7th percentile, near 10-year lows).","implication":"CRITICAL INSIGHT: Office absorption is rising but NOT driven by traditional IT services — it's Global Capability Centers (GCCs) and non-IT corporates (BFSI, consulting, startups). IT services are consolidating footprints (hence flat stock prices despite rising rents). This decoupling signals: (1) Overweight commercial REITs (Embassy, Mindspace) — they're capturing GCC demand, not IT services demand. (2) Underweight Nifty IT — their office real estate is becoming a liability, not an asset. (3) Airline traffic collapse (-9.1%) contradicts office absorption surge — suggests office users are local/remote-hybrid, not business travel dependent. This is a structural shift from IT-centric to distributed corporate real estate.","pct_10y":24,"pct_note":"Office absorption at 24th percentile is historically low, but the COMPOSITION has shifted. Last time office absorption was this low (2020-21), it was driven by IT services contraction. Today, it's driven by GCC expansion — a fundamentally different dynamic that REITs are capturing but IT stocks are not.","is_surprise":true}

executive_summary.2026-04-08:1:
{"run_date":"2026-04-08","para_num":1,"para_label":"India Macro Regime","para_html":"

GDP 7.8% with PMI Composite at 57 puts India in unambiguous expansion — 3 of 6 macro dimensions are in growth mode simultaneously. GST collections at ₹2,00,064 Cr YoY alongside PV sales growth of 10.5% confirm demand is broad-based, not sector-specific. CPI at 3.21% with fuel deflation at -3.78% gives RBI room to hold the repo at 5.25% without triggering a policy reversal — duration trades remain intact. Credit growth at 14.3% against deposit growth of 10.9% — CD ratio now 83.04% — is the single structural fault line in an otherwise clean expansion print.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-08:2:
{"run_date":"2026-04-08","para_num":2,"para_label":"Global Macro Regime","para_html":"

S&P 500 at 6,779 (+2.5%) and Nasdaq at 22,653 (+3.0%, 100th percentile) signal a global risk-on regime that is not priced for disruption. Brent at $94.98/bbl — down 13% from $109.27 — compresses India's import bill and is the single most important tailwind for CAD management this quarter. Copper at $5.766/lb (95th percentile, 10-year range) and iron ore surging to $161.91/t from $108.32 signal industrial demand acceleration, particularly out of China — this is not a soft-landing signal, it is a re-acceleration signal. DXY at 98.84 and US 10Y at 4.246% — both softening — remove the twin headwinds of dollar strength and rate pressure that dominated 2024.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-08:3:
{"run_date":"2026-04-08","para_num":3,"para_label":"Liquidity Conditions","para_html":"

SIP AUM at ₹16.64 Tr on 9.92 Cr accounts is structural bedrock — a -3.7% MoM dip in SIP inflows to ₹29,845 Cr is noise against 15% YoY growth. Equity MF net inflows accelerated to ₹25,978 Cr (prev: ₹24,029 Cr), and NFO collections exploded to ₹3,955 Cr from ₹806 Cr — DII buying is not slowing, it is rotating into new paper. India VIX collapsing 20.25% to 19.70 from 24.7 — concurrent with US VIX falling to 21.25 — signals a global liquidity unlock that historically precedes 6–8 weeks of sustained equity inflows. FII positioning remains the swing variable; INR stability at 92.59 against a weakening DXY (98.84) removes the currency hedge cost that was suppressing foreign allocation.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-08:4:
{"run_date":"2026-04-08","para_num":4,"para_label":"Equity + Real Estate Implications","para_html":"

Nifty50 at 23,997 (+3.78%) and Sensex at 77,563 (+3.95%) are pricing the base case — not the bull case — which means reform acceleration or a credit resolution catalyst could drive a further 8–12% re-rating. Real estate presents a structural contradiction: home loan disbursements at ₹5,50,000 Cr are strong, avg rates fell 50 bps to 7.65%, yet RE sales declined 6.7% to 1,01,675 units against an affordability index deteriorating 32% to 0.61 — volume is being bought by rate cuts, not by income growth. Office REITs are the cleaner play: absorption at 83.3 (+13.7%) with vacancy compressing to 15.2% — Embassy REIT at ₹429.85 and Mindspace at ₹449.98 are at 75–79th percentiles, not stretched. Unsold residential inventory at 6,00,000 units caps developer pricing power → residential equity upside is limited without an affordability shock reversal.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-08:5:
{"run_date":"2026-04-08","para_num":5,"para_label":"Key Risks to Monitor","para_html":"

Ranked by probability × impact: #1 — CD ratio at 83.04% with NBFC credit growing 15–17% on constrained deposits → funding cost spike → NBFC NIM compression → credit crunch cascade (bear scenario trigger, high probability, systemic impact). #2 — Affordability index at 0.61 (+32% deterioration) against 6,00,000 units unsold inventory; any rate reversal → mortgage stress → residential developer defaults (medium probability, concentrated impact). #3 — Copper at 95th percentile and iron ore at $161.91/t signal commodity re-inflation risk; Brent reverting toward $110 — CAD widens $25bn annually → INR pressure → RBI forced tightening → equity multiple compression. Watch the CD ratio print for April — at 83.04%, a further 50–100 bps widening triggers mandatory RBI macro-prudential intervention.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-09:1:
{"run_date":"2026-04-09","para_num":1,"para_label":"India Macro Regime","para_html":"

7.8% GDP with PMI composite at 57 and GST breaching ₹2L crore for the first time constructs an irresistible headline — but three proxies undercut it: core sector at 2.3% (half its prior reading), airline passengers at a decade-low 7th percentile, and e-commerce GMV growth collapsing from 25-30% to 12.4%. RBI's own forecast of 6.9% sits 90bps below the print — that gap is either a deflator anomaly or a GVA-GDP statistical artefact, neither of which represents real output. Manufacturing PMI slipped to 53.9 from 56.9 while services held at 57.5 — India's growth engine is increasingly a services story, which employs fewer people per rupee of output than manufacturing. 7.8% is the number; whether it is the economy is a different question.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-09:2:
{"run_date":"2026-04-09","para_num":2,"para_label":"Global Macro Regime","para_html":"

US GDP at +0.7% SAAR — half the prior quarter's 1.4% — while core PCE ticked up to 3.1% from 3.0%: stagflation's shadow is lengthening even as equity markets price perfection, with S&P 500 at a 96th-percentile 6,782 and Nasdaq at an all-time high 22,635. Copper at a 94th-percentile $5.716/lb alongside iron ore surging 49.5% to $161.91 signals genuine industrial demand — most likely China-driven — that contradicts the US slowdown narrative and keeps global inflation risks alive. Brent's 11.5% collapse to $96.84 is India's single largest external gift this quarter — every $10 drop saves approximately $15bn on the import bill — but at $96.84, oil is still above the $85 level where India's CAD math becomes comfortable. DXY at 98.97 — a 46th-percentile reading — gives EM currencies room to breathe, but US core PCE re-accelerating forecloses Fed cuts and keeps the carry trade calculus unfavorable for India.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-09:3:
{"run_date":"2026-04-09","para_num":3,"para_label":"Liquidity Conditions","para_html":"

India VIX at 19.7 — down from 24.7, now at a 15th-percentile calm — masks the structural liquidity stress underneath: CD ratio at 83.04% with deposit growth at 10.8% against credit at 14.3% means banks are lending rupees they haven't yet collected. SIP inflows at ₹29,845 crore fell 3.7% MoM from ₹31,002 crore — the first meaningful deceleration in the retail savings pipeline — while FII equity outflows of ₹8,692 crore were almost exactly offset by DII inflows of ₹7,980 crore, a ₹712 crore net drain that the market absorbed without distress only because VIX compressed simultaneously. Corporate bond issuance at ₹5.5L crore in nine months versus ₹6.0L crore for all of FY22 signals that the wholesale funding market is being tapped urgently — refinancing pressure, not growth appetite, is driving issuance. If deposit growth doesn't recover to 12%+ within two quarters, the RBI faces a binary: tighten macro-prudential screws or watch the CD ratio breach 85% — a level with no modern precedent in India's banking system.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-09:4:
{"run_date":"2026-04-09","para_num":4,"para_label":"Equity + Real Estate Implications","para_html":"

Nifty 50 at 23,997 (77th percentile) and Bank Nifty at 55,704 (92nd percentile) price a benign credit cycle — but Bank Nifty at the 92nd percentile while CD ratio sits at the 81st percentile is a valuation tension that resolves badly: NIM compression from deposit rate wars → earnings downgrades → derating, with the timing determined by when wholesale funding costs visibly spike. Real estate shows a clean divergence: residential launches up 2% to 126,265 units while sales fell 6.7% to 101,675 units — unsold inventory jumped to 600,000 units from 560 (a data anomaly in the prior figure, but directionally a supply overhang building). Office REITs tell a more interesting story — Embassy at 78th percentile, Mindspace at 79th percentile, absorption up 5.9% to 29.9mn sqft with vacancy falling to 13.9% — yet Nifty IT is flat YoY, suggesting the market doesn't believe the office demand is tech-sector-driven, or that it's GCC and BFSI absorption masking IT headcount stagnation. REITs offer a cleaner expression of the office recovery than IT equities at current valuations.

"}
executive_summary.2026-04-09:5:
{"run_date":"2026-04-09","para_num":5,"para_label":"Key Risks to Monitor","para_html":"

Ranked by probability × impact: first, CD ratio breach of 85% — probability high within two quarters if deposit growth stays at 10.8% — triggering macro-prudential tightening that reprices NBFC credit and compresses Bank Nifty earnings by an estimated 8-12%. Second, food inflation compounding — CFPI at 3.47% from 2.13% in one month, with FAO food index at 128.5 and rising — if the monsoon disappoints by even 5% below normal, RBI's 4.6% inflation forecast becomes a floor, not a ceiling, closing the rate-cut window entirely. Third, US stagflation feedback — GDP at 0.7% SAAR with core PCE at 3.1% keeps the Fed on hold, sustains US 10Y at 4.31%, and removes the EM carry trade tailwind that has kept INR stable at 92.59 despite ₹8,692 crore of FII equity outflows. Fourth, the airline passenger anomaly — 35.08mn at the 7th percentile is either a data artifact or the sharpest leading indicator of upper-income consumption stress in today's dataset; watch April traffic numbers as the single most important data point this week.

"}
